advantaged, and the corrupt deputy scaled. The maid
will I frame and make fit for his attempt. If you
think well to carry this as you may, the doubleness
of the benefit defends the deceit from reproof.
What think you of it?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
This forenamed maid hath yet in her the continuance
of her first affection: his unjust unkindness, that
in all reason should have quenched her love, hath,
like an impediment in the current, made it more
violent and unruly. Go you to Angelo; answer his
requiring with a plausible obedience; agree with
his demands to the point; only refer yourself to
this advantage, first, that your stay with him may
not be long; that the time may have all shadow and
silence in it; and the place answer to convenience.
This being granted in course,—and now follows
all,—we shall advise this wronged maid to stead up
your appointment, go in your place; if the encounter
acknowledge itself hereafter, it may compel him to
her recompense: and here, by this, is your brother
saved, your honour untainted, the poor Mariana
advantaged, and the corrupt deputy scaled. The maid
will I frame and make fit for his attempt. If you
think well to carry this as you may, the doubleness
of the benefit defends the deceit from reproof.
What think you of it?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
The image of it gives me content already; and I
trust it will grow to a most prosperous perfection.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
The image of it gives me content already; and I
trust it will grow to a most prosperous perfection.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
It lies much in your holding up. Haste you speedily
to Angelo: if for this night he entreat you to his
bed, give him promise of satisfaction. I will
presently to Saint Luke's: there, at the moated
grange, resides this dejected Mariana. At that
place call upon me; and dispatch with Angelo, that
it may be quickly.

I thank you for this comfort. Fare you well, good
father.

Enter, on one side, DUKE VINCENTIO disguised as before; on the other, ELBOW, and Officers with POMPEY
Enter, on one side, DUKE VINCENTIO disguised as before; on the other, ELBOW, and Officers with POMPEY

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text:
Nay, if there be no remedy for it, but that you will
needs buy and sell men and women like beasts, we
shall have all the world drink brown and white bastard.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: O heavens! what stuff is here?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: O heavens! what stuff is here?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
'Twas never merry world since, of two usuries, the
merriest was put down, and the worser allowed by
order of law a furred gown to keep him warm; and
furred with fox and lamb-skins too, to signify, that
craft, being richer than innocency, stands for the
facing.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text: Come your way, sir. 'Bless you, good father friar.

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text: Come your way, sir. 'Bless you, good father friar.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
And you, good brother father. What offence hath
this man made you, sir?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
And you, good brother father. What offence hath
this man made you, sir?

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, he hath offended the law: and, sir, we
take him to be a thief too, sir; for we have found
upon him, sir, a strange picklock, which we have
sent to the deputy.

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, he hath offended the law: and, sir, we
take him to be a thief too, sir; for we have found
upon him, sir, a strange picklock, which we have
sent to the deputy.

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, he hath offended the law: and, sir, we

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Fie, sirrah! a bawd, a wicked bawd!
The evil that thou causest to be done,
That is thy means to live. Do thou but think
What 'tis to cram a maw or clothe a back
From such a filthy vice: say to thyself,
From their abominable and beastly touches
I drink, I eat, array myself, and live.
Canst thou believe thy living is a life,
So stinkingly depending? Go mend, go mend.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Indeed, it does stink in some sort, sir; but yet,
sir, I would prove—

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Indeed, it does stink in some sort, sir; but yet,
sir, I would prove—

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Indeed, it does stink in some sort, sir; but yet,
sir, I would prove—

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Nay, if the devil have given thee proofs for sin,
Thou wilt prove his. Take him to prison, officer:
Correction and instruction must both work
Ere this rude beast will profit.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Nay, if the devil have given thee proofs for sin,

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text:
He must before the deputy, sir; he has given him
warning: the deputy cannot abide a whoremaster: if
he be a whoremonger, and comes before him, he were
as good go a mile on his errand.

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text:

warning: the deputy cannot abide a whoremaster: if
he be a whoremonger, and comes before him, he were
as good go a mile on his errand.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
That we were all, as some would seem to be,
From our faults, as faults from seeming, free!

sentences.speaker: ELBOW
sentences.text: His neck will come to your waist,—a cord, sir.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
I spy comfort; I cry bail. Here's a gentleman and a
friend of mine.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
I spy comfort; I cry bail. Here's a gentleman and a
friend of mine.
Enter LUCIO

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
How now, noble Pompey! What, at the wheels of
Caesar? art thou led in triumph? What, is there
none of Pygmalion's images, newly made woman, to be
had now, for putting the hand in the pocket and
extracting it clutch'd? What reply, ha? What
sayest thou to this tune, matter and method? Is't
not drowned i' the last rain, ha? What sayest
thou, Trot? Is the world as it was, man? Which is
the way? Is it sad, and few words? or how? The
trick of it?

thou, Trot? Is the world as it was, man? Which is
the way? Is it sad, and few words? or how? The
trick of it?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
How now, noble Pompey! What, at the wheels of
Caesar? art thou led in triumph? What, is there
none of Pygmalion's images, newly made woman, to be
had now, for putting the hand in the pocket and
extracting it clutch'd? What reply, ha? What
sayest thou to this tune, matter and method? Is't
not drowned i' the last rain, ha? What sayest
thou, Trot? Is the world as it was, man? Which is
the way? Is it sad, and few words? or how? The
trick of it?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Still thus, and thus; still worse!

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
How doth my dear morsel, thy mistress? Procures she
still, ha?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
How doth my dear morsel, thy mistress? Procures she
still, ha?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Troth, sir, she hath eaten up all her beef, and she
is herself in the tub.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Why, 'tis good; it is the right of it; it must be
so: ever your fresh whore and your powdered bawd:
an unshunned consequence; it must be so. Art going
to prison, Pompey?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Well, then, imprison him: if imprisonment be the
due of a bawd, why, 'tis his right: bawd is he
doubtless, and of antiquity too; bawd-born.
Farewell, good Pompey. Commend me to the prison,
Pompey: you will turn good husband now, Pompey; you
will keep the house.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: I hope, sir, your good worship will be my bail.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
No, indeed, will I not, Pompey; it is not the wear.
I will pray, Pompey, to increase your bondage: If
you take it not patiently, why, your mettle is the
more. Adieu, trusty Pompey. 'Bless you, friar.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Does Bridget paint still, Pompey, ha?

sentences.text: You will not bail me, then, sir?

what news?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Go to kennel, Pompey; go.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: What news, friar, of the duke?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: I know none. Can you tell me of any?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: I know none. Can you tell me of any?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Some say he is with the Emperor of Russia; other
some, he is in Rome: but where is he, think you?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO

some, he is in Rome: but where is he, think you?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: I know not where; but wheresoever, I wish him well.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: I know not where; but wheresoever, I wish him well.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
It was a mad fantastical trick of him to steal from
the state, and usurp the beggary he was never born
to. Lord Angelo dukes it well in his absence; he
puts transgression to 't.

sentences.text:
A little more lenity to lechery would do no harm in
him: something too crabbed that way, friar.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
A little more lenity to lechery would do no harm in

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: It is too general a vice, and severity must cure it.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Yes, in good sooth, the vice is of a great kindred;
it is well allied: but it is impossible to extirp
it quite, friar, till eating and drinking be put
down. They say this Angelo was not made by man and
woman after this downright way of creation: is it
true, think you?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: How should he be made, then?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Some report a sea-maid spawned him; some, that he
was begot between two stock-fishes. But it is
certain that when he makes water his urine is
congealed ice; that I know to be true: and he is a
motion generative; that's infallible.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: You are pleasant, sir, and speak apace.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Why, what a ruthless thing is this in him, for the
rebellion of a codpiece to take away the life of a
man! Would the duke that is absent have done this?
Ere he would have hanged a man for the getting a
hundred bastards, he would have paid for the nursing
a thousand: he had some feeling of the sport: he
knew the service, and that instructed him to mercy.

sentences.text:
I never heard the absent duke much detected for
women; he was not inclined that way.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: O, sir, you are deceived.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: 'Tis not possible.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Who, not the duke? yes, your beggar of fifty; and
his use was to put a ducat in her clack-dish: the
duke had crotchets in him. He would be drunk too;
that let me inform you.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: You do him wrong, surely.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Sir, I was an inward of his. A shy fellow was the
duke: and I believe I know the cause of his
withdrawing.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: What, I prithee, might be the cause?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
No, pardon; 'tis a secret must be locked within the
teeth and the lips: but this I can let you
understand, the greater file of the subject held the
duke to be wise.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Wise! why, no question but he was.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Wise! why, no question but he was.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: A very superficial, ignorant, unweighing fellow.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Either this is the envy in you, folly, or mistaking:
the very stream of his life and the business he hath
helmed must upon a warranted need give him a better
proclamation. Let him be but testimonied in his own
bringings-forth, and he shall appear to the
envious a scholar, a statesman and a soldier.
Therefore you speak unskilfully: or if your
knowledge be more it is much darkened in your malice.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Sir, I know him, and I love him.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Love talks with better knowledge, and knowledge with
dearer love.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Love talks with better knowledge, and knowledge with

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Come, sir, I know what I know.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
I can hardly believe that, since you know not what
you speak. But, if ever the duke return, as our
prayers are he may, let me desire you to make your
answer before him. If it be honest you have spoke,
you have courage to maintain it: I am bound to call
upon you; and, I pray you, your name?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Sir, my name is Lucio; well known to the duke.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
He shall know you better, sir, if I may live to
report you.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: I fear you not.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
O, you hope the duke will return no more; or you
imagine me too unhurtful an opposite. But indeed I
can do you little harm; you'll forswear this again.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
I'll be hanged first: thou art deceived in me,
friar. But no more of this. Canst thou tell if
Claudio die to-morrow or no?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Why should he die, sir?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Why? For filling a bottle with a tundish. I would
the duke we talk of were returned again: the
ungenitured agent will unpeople the province with
continency; sparrows must not build in his
house-eaves, because they are lecherous. The duke
yet would have dark deeds darkly answered; he would
never bring them to light: would he were returned!
Marry, this Claudio is condemned for untrussing.
Farewell, good friar: I prithee, pray for me. The
duke, I say to thee again, would eat mutton on
Fridays. He's not past it yet, and I say to thee,
he would mouth with a beggar, though she smelt brown
bread and garlic: say that I said so. Farewell.

yet would have dark deeds darkly answered; he would
never bring them to light: would he were returned!
Marry, this Claudio is condemned for untrussing.
Farewell, good friar: I prithee, pray for me. The
duke, I say to thee again, would eat mutton on
Fridays. He's not past it yet, and I say to thee,
he would mouth with a beggar, though she smelt brown
bread and garlic: say that I said so. Farewell.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
No might nor greatness in mortality
Can censure 'scape; back-wounding calumny
The whitest virtue strikes. What king so strong
Can tie the gall up in the slanderous tongue?
But who comes here?

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS OVERDONE
sentences.text:
Good my lord, be good to me; your honour is accounted
a merciful man; good my lord.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST

A bawd of eleven years' continuance, may it please
your honour.

sentences.speaker: MISTRESS OVERDONE
sentences.text:
My lord, this is one Lucio's information against me.
Mistress Kate Keepdown was with child by him in the
duke's time; he promised her marriage: his child
is a year and a quarter old, come Philip and Jacob:
I have kept it myself; and see how he goes about to
abuse me!

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Provost, my brother Angelo will not be altered;
Claudio must die to-morrow: let him be furnished
with divines, and have all charitable preparation.
if my brother wrought by my pity, it should not be
so with him.

So please you, this friar hath been with him, and
advised him for the entertainment of death.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: Good even, good father.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Bliss and goodness on you!

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: Of whence are you?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Not of this country, though my chance is now
To use it for my time: I am a brother
Of gracious order, late come from the See
In special business from his holiness.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: What news abroad i' the world?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
None, but that there is so great a fever on
goodness, that the dissolution of it must cure it:
novelty is only in request; and it is as dangerous
to be aged in any kind of course, as it is virtuous
to be constant in any undertaking. There is scarce
truth enough alive to make societies secure; but
security enough to make fellowships accurst: much
upon this riddle runs the wisdom of the world. This
news is old enough, yet it is every day's news. I
pray you, sir, of what disposition was the duke?

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
One that, above all other strifes, contended
especially to know himself.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: What pleasure was he given to?

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Rather rejoicing to see another merry, than merry at
any thing which professed to make him rejoice: a
gentleman of all temperance. But leave we him to
his events, with a prayer they may prove prosperous;
and let me desire to know how you find Claudio
prepared. I am made to understand that you have
lent him visitation.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
He professes to have received no sinister measure
from his judge, but most willingly humbles himself
to the determination of justice: yet had he framed
to himself, by the instruction of his frailty, many
deceiving promises of life; which I by my good
leisure have discredited to him, and now is he
resolved to die.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
You have paid the heavens your function, and the
prisoner the very debt of your calling. I have
laboured for the poor gentleman to the extremest
shore of my modesty: but my brother justice have I
found so severe, that he hath forced me to tell him
he is indeed Justice.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
If his own life answer the straitness of his
proceeding, it shall become him well; wherein if he
chance to fail, he hath sentenced himself.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: I am going to visit the prisoner. Fare you well.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: I am going to visit the prisoner. Fare you well.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Peace be with you!
Exeunt ESCALUS and Provost

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
He who the sword of heaven will bear
Should be as holy as severe;
Pattern in himself to know,
Grace to stand, and virtue go;
More nor less to others paying
Than by self-offences weighing.
Shame to him whose cruel striking
Kills for faults of his own liking!
Twice treble shame on Angelo,
To weed my vice and let his grow!
O, what may man within him hide,
Though angel on the outward side!
How may likeness made in crimes,
Making practise on the times,
To draw with idle spiders' strings
Most ponderous and substantial things!
Craft against vice I must apply:
With Angelo to-night shall lie
His old betrothed but despised;
So disguise shall, by the disguised,
Pay with falsehood false exacting,
And perform an old contracting.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
He who the sword of heaven will bear
Should be as holy as severe;
Pattern in himself to know,
Grace to stand, and virtue go;
More nor less to others paying
Than by self-offences weighing.
Shame to him whose cruel striking
Kills for faults of his own liking!
Twice treble shame on Angelo,
To weed my vice and let his grow!
O, what may man within him hide,
Though angel on the outward side!
How may likeness made in crimes,
Making practise on the times,
To draw with idle spiders' strings
Most ponderous and substantial things!
Craft against vice I must apply:
With Angelo to-night shall lie
His old betrothed but despised;
So disguise shall, by the disguised,
Pay with falsehood false exacting,
And perform an old contracting.

BOY

sentences.text: Enter DUKE VINCENTIO disguised as before

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
Break off thy song, and haste thee quick away:
Here comes a man of comfort, whose advice
Hath often still'd my brawling discontent.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
I cry you mercy, sir; and well could wish
You had not found me here so musical:
Let me excuse me, and believe me so,
My mirth it much displeased, but pleased my woe.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
'Tis good; though music oft hath such a charm
To make bad good, and good provoke to harm.
I pray, you, tell me, hath any body inquired
for me here to-day? much upon this time have
I promised here to meet.

I do constantly believe you. The time is come even
now. I shall crave your forbearance a little: may
be I will call upon you anon, for some advantage to
yourself.

Very well met, and well come.
What is the news from this good deputy?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
He hath a garden circummured with brick,
Whose western side is with a vineyard back'd;
And to that vineyard is a planched gate,
That makes his opening with this bigger key:
This other doth command a little door
Which from the vineyard to the garden leads;
There have I made my promise
Upon the heavy middle of the night
To call upon him.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: But shall you on your knowledge find this way?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
I have ta'en a due and wary note upon't:
With whispering and most guilty diligence,
In action all of precept, he did show me
The way twice o'er.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Are there no other tokens
Between you 'greed concerning her observance?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
No, none, but only a repair i' the dark;
And that I have possess'd him my most stay
Can be but brief; for I have made him know
I have a servant comes with me along,
That stays upon me, whose persuasion is
I come about my brother.

I pray you, be acquainted with this maid;

sentences.speaker: MARIANA

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO

Take, then, this your companion by the hand,
Who hath a story ready for your ear.
I shall attend your leisure: but make haste;
The vaporous night approaches.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
O place and greatness! millions of false eyes
Are stuck upon thee: volumes of report
Run with these false and most contrarious quests
Upon thy doings: thousand escapes of wit
Make thee the father of their idle dreams
And rack thee in their fancies.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Welcome, how agreed?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Welcome, how agreed?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
She'll take the enterprise upon her, father,
If you advise it.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
It is not my consent,
But my entreaty too.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
Little have you to say
When you depart from him, but, soft and low,
"Remember now my brother."

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text: Fear me not.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Nor, gentle daughter, fear you not at all.
He is your husband on a pre-contract:
To bring you thus together, 'tis no sin,
Sith that the justice of your title to him
Doth flourish the deceit. Come, let us go:
Our corn's to reap, for yet our tithe's to sow.

Enter Provost and POMPEY

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Come hither, sirrah. Can you cut off a man's head?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Come hither, sirrah. Can you cut off a man's head?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
If the man be a bachelor, sir, I can; but if he be a
married man, he's his wife's head, and I can never
cut off a woman's head.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
If the man be a bachelor, sir, I can; but if he be a
married man, he's his wife's head, and I can never
cut off a woman's head.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Come, sir, leave me your snatches, and yield me a
direct answer. To-morrow morning are to die Claudio
and Barnardine. Here is in our prison a common
executioner, who in his office lacks a helper: if
you will take it on you to assist him, it shall
redeem you from your gyves; if not, you shall have
your full time of imprisonment and your deliverance
with an unpitied whipping, for you have been a
notorious bawd.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Sir, I have been an unlawful bawd time out of mind;
but yet I will be content to be a lawful hangman. I
would be glad to receive some instruction from my
fellow partner.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: What, ho! Abhorson! Where's Abhorson, there?

Enter ABHORSON

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text: Do you call, sir?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Sirrah, here's a fellow will help you to-morrow in
your execution. If you think it meet, compound with
him by the year, and let him abide here with you; if
not, use him for the present and dismiss him. He
cannot plead his estimation with you; he hath been a
bawd.

mystery.

the scale.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Pray, sir, by your good favour,—for surely, sir, a
good favour you have, but that you have a hanging
look,—do you call, sir, your occupation a mystery?

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text: Ay, sir; a mystery

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Painting, sir, I have heard say, is a mystery; and
your whores, sir, being members of my occupation,
using painting, do prove my occupation a mystery:
but what mystery there should be in hanging, if I
should be hanged, I cannot imagine.

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text:
Every true man's apparel fits your thief: if it be
too little for your thief, your true man thinks it
big enough; if it be too big for your thief, your
thief thinks it little enough: so every true man's
apparel fits your thief.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Sir, I will serve him; for I do find your hangman is
a more penitent trade than your bawd; he doth
oftener ask forgiveness.

Come on, bawd; I will instruct thee in my trade;
follow.

me yare; for truly, sir, for your kindness I owe you
a good turn.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
The one has my pity; not a jot the other,
Being a murderer, though he were my brother.
Enter CLAUDIO

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Look, here's the warrant, Claudio, for thy death:
'Tis now dead midnight, and by eight to-morrow
Thou must be made immortal. Where's Barnardine?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Look, here's the warrant, Claudio, for thy death:
'Tis now dead midnight, and by eight to-morrow
Thou must be made immortal. Where's Barnardine?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Look, here's the warrant, Claudio, for thy death:
'Tis now dead midnight, and by eight to-morrow
Thou must be made immortal. Where's Barnardine?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text:
As fast lock'd up in sleep as guiltless labour
When it lies starkly in the traveller's bones:
He will not wake.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIO
sentences.text:
As fast lock'd up in sleep as guiltless labour
When it lies starkly in the traveller's bones:
He will not wake.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Who can do good on him?
Well, go, prepare yourself.
[Knocking within]
But, hark, what noise?
Heaven give your spirits comfort!

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Who can do good on him?
Well, go, prepare yourself.
[Knocking within]
But, hark, what noise?
Heaven give your spirits comfort!

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Who can do good on him?
Well, go, prepare yourself.
[Knocking within]
But, hark, what noise?
Heaven give your spirits comfort!
Exit CLAUDIO

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
By and by.
I hope it is some pardon or reprieve
For the most gentle Claudio.
Enter DUKE VINCENTIO disguised as before

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Welcome father.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
The best and wholesomest spirts of the night
Envelope you, good Provost! Who call'd here of late?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
The best and wholesomest spirts of the night
Envelope you, good Provost! Who call'd here of late?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
The best and wholesomest spirts of the night
Envelope you, good Provost! Who call'd here of late?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: None, since the curfew rung.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Not Isabel?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: No.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: They will, then, ere't be long.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: What comfort is for Claudio?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: There's some in hope.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: It is a bitter deputy.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Not so, not so; his life is parallel'd
Even with the stroke and line of his great justice:
He doth with holy abstinence subdue
That in himself which he spurs on his power
To qualify in others: were he meal'd with that
Which he corrects, then were he tyrannous;
But this being so, he's just.
[Knocking within]
Now are they come.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
This is a gentle provost: seldom when
The steeled gaoler is the friend of men.
[Knocking within]
How now! what noise? That spirit's possessed with haste
That wounds the unsisting postern with these strokes.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
There he must stay until the officer
Arise to let him in: he is call'd up.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
There he must stay until the officer
Arise to let him in: he is call'd up.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Have you no countermand for Claudio yet,
But he must die to-morrow?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Have you no countermand for Claudio yet,
But he must die to-morrow?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: None, sir, none.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
As near the dawning, provost, as it is,
You shall hear more ere morning.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Happily
You something know; yet I believe there comes
No countermand; no such example have we:
Besides, upon the very siege of justice
Lord Angelo hath to the public ear
Profess'd the contrary.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:

Enter a MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: This is his lordship's man.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: And here comes Claudio's pardon.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
[Giving a paper]
My lord hath sent you this note; and by me this
further charge, that you swerve not from the
smallest article of it, neither in time, matter, or
other circumstance. Good morrow; for, as I take it,
it is almost day.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
[Giving a paper]

smallest article of it, neither in time, matter, or

it is almost day.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: I shall obey him.
Exit Messenger

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
[aside] This is his pardon, purchased by such sin
For which the pardoner himself is in.
Hence hath offence his quick celerity,
When it is born in high authority:
When vice makes mercy, mercy's so extended,
That for the fault's love is the offender friended.
Now, sir, what news?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
I told you. Lord Angelo, belike thinking me remiss
in mine office, awakens me with this unwonted
putting-on; methinks strangely, for he hath not used it
before.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
I told you. Lord Angelo, belike thinking me remiss
in mine office, awakens me with this unwonted

before.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Pray you, let's hear.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
[Reads]
'Whatsoever you may hear to the contrary, let
Claudio be executed by four of the clock; and in the
afternoon Barnardine: for my better satisfaction,
let me have Claudio's head sent me by five. Let
this be duly performed; with a thought that more
depends on it than we must yet deliver. Thus fail
not to do your office, as you will answer it at your
peril.'
What say you to this, sir?

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
What is that Barnardine who is to be executed in the
afternoon?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
A Bohemian born, but here nursed un and bred; one
that is a prisoner nine years old.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
A Bohemian born, but here nursed un and bred; one
that is a prisoner nine years old.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
How came it that the absent duke had not either
delivered him to his liberty or executed him? I
have heard it was ever his manner to do so.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
How came it that the absent duke had not either
delivered him to his liberty or executed him? I
have heard it was ever his manner to do so.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
His friends still wrought reprieves for him: and,
indeed, his fact, till now in the government of Lord
Angelo, came not to an undoubtful proof.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
His friends still wrought reprieves for him: and,
indeed, his fact, till now in the government of Lord
Angelo, came not to an undoubtful proof.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: It is now apparent?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Most manifest, and not denied by himself.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Most manifest, and not denied by himself.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Hath he born himself penitently in prison? how
seems he to be touched?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Hath he born himself penitently in prison? how
seems he to be touched?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
A man that apprehends death no more dreadfully but
as a drunken sleep; careless, reckless, and fearless
of what's past, present, or to come; insensible of
mortality, and desperately mortal.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
A man that apprehends death no more dreadfully but
as a drunken sleep; careless, reckless, and fearless
of what's past, present, or to come; insensible of
mortality, and desperately mortal.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: He wants advice.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
He will hear none: he hath evermore had the liberty
of the prison; give him leave to escape hence, he
would not: drunk many times a day, if not many days
entirely drunk. We have very oft awaked him, as if
to carry him to execution, and showed him a seeming
warrant for it: it hath not moved him at all.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
More of him anon. There is written in your brow,
provost, honesty and constancy: if I read it not
truly, my ancient skill beguiles me; but, in the
boldness of my cunning, I will lay myself in hazard.
Claudio, whom here you have warrant to execute, is
no greater forfeit to the law than Angelo who hath
sentenced him. To make you understand this in a
manifested effect, I crave but four days' respite;
for the which you are to do me both a present and a
dangerous courtesy.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Pray, sir, in what?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: In the delaying death.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
A lack, how may I do it, having the hour limited,
and an express command, under penalty, to deliver
his head in the view of Angelo? I may make my case
as Claudio's, to cross this in the smallest.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
A lack, how may I do it, having the hour limited,
and an express command, under penalty, to deliver
his head in the view of Angelo? I may make my case
as Claudio's, to cross this in the smallest.

sentences.text:
A lack, how may I do it, having the hour limited,

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
By the vow of mine order I warrant you, if my
instructions may be your guide. Let this Barnardine
be this morning executed, and his head born to Angelo.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
By the vow of mine order I warrant you, if my
instructions may be your guide. Let this Barnardine
be this morning executed, and his head born to Angelo.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
Angelo hath seen them both, and will discover the
favour.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
O, death's a great disguiser; and you may add to it.
Shave the head, and tie the beard; and say it was
the desire of the penitent to be so bared before his
death: you know the course is common. If any thing
fall to you upon this, more than thanks and good
fortune, by the saint whom I profess, I will plead
against it with my life.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Pardon me, good father; it is against my oath.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Pardon me, good father; it is against my oath.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Were you sworn to the duke, or to the deputy?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: To him, and to his substitutes.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
You will think you have made no offence, if the duke
avouch the justice of your dealing?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: But what likelihood is in that?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Not a resemblance, but a certainty. Yet since I see
you fearful, that neither my coat, integrity, nor
persuasion can with ease attempt you, I will go
further than I meant, to pluck all fears out of you.
Look you, sir, here is the hand and seal of the
duke: you know the character, I doubt not; and the
signet is not strange to you.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Not a resemblance, but a certainty. Yet since I see
you fearful, that neither my coat, integrity, nor

further than I meant, to pluck all fears out of you.
Look you, sir, here is the hand and seal of the
duke: you know the character, I doubt not; and the
signet is not strange to you.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: I know them both.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
The contents of this is the return of the duke: you
shall anon over-read it at your pleasure; where you
shall find, within these two days he will be here.
This is a thing that Angelo knows not; for he this
very day receives letters of strange tenor;
perchance of the duke's death; perchance entering
into some monastery; but, by chance, nothing of what
is writ. Look, the unfolding star calls up the
shepherd. Put not yourself into amazement how these
things should be: all difficulties are but easy
when they are known. Call your executioner, and off
with Barnardine's head: I will give him a present
shrift and advise him for a better place. Yet you
are amazed; but this shall absolutely resolve you.
Come away; it is almost clear dawn.

shrift and advise him for a better place. Yet you
are amazed; but this shall absolutely resolve you.
Come away; it is almost clear dawn.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
The contents of this is the return of the duke: you
shall anon over-read it at your pleasure; where you
shall find, within these two days he will be here.
This is a thing that Angelo knows not; for he this
very day receives letters of strange tenor;
perchance of the duke's death; perchance entering
into some monastery; but, by chance, nothing of what
is writ. Look, the unfolding star calls up the
shepherd. Put not yourself into amazement how these
things should be: all difficulties are but easy
when they are known. Call your executioner, and off
with Barnardine's head: I will give him a present
shrift and advise him for a better place. Yet you
are amazed; but this shall absolutely resolve you.
Come away; it is almost clear dawn.

Enter POMPEY

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
I am as well acquainted here as I was in our house
of profession: one would think it were Mistress
Overdone's own house, for here be many of her old
customers. First, here's young Master Rash; he's in
for a commodity of brown paper and old ginger,
ninescore and seventeen pounds; of which he made
five marks, ready money: marry, then ginger was not
much in request, for the old women were all dead.
Then is there here one Master Caper, at the suit of
Master Three-pile the mercer, for some four suits of
peach-coloured satin, which now peaches him a
beggar. Then have we here young Dizy, and young
Master Deep-vow, and Master Copperspur, and Master
Starve-lackey the rapier and dagger man, and young
Drop-heir that killed lusty Pudding, and Master
Forthlight the tilter, and brave Master Shooty the
great traveller, and wild Half-can that stabbed
Pots, and, I think, forty more; all great doers in
our trade, and are now "for the Lord's sake."

Enter ABHORSON

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text: Sirrah, bring Barnardine hither.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Master Barnardine! you must rise and be hanged.
Master Barnardine!

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Master Barnardine! you must rise and be hanged.
Master Barnardine!

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Master Barnardine! you must rise and be hanged.
Master Barnardine!

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Master Barnardine! you must rise and be hanged.
Master Barnardine!

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Master Barnardine! you must rise and be hanged.
Master Barnardine!

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text: What, ho, Barnardine!

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
[Within] A pox o' your throats! Who makes that
noise there? What are you?

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
[Within] A pox o' your throats! Who makes that
noise there? What are you?

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
[Within] A pox o' your throats! Who makes that
noise there? What are you?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Your friends, sir; the hangman. You must be so
good, sir, to rise and be put to death.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Your friends, sir; the hangman. You must be so
good, sir, to rise and be put to death.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text: [Within] Away, you rogue, away! I am sleepy.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text: [Within] Away, you rogue, away! I am sleepy.

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text: Tell him he must awake, and that quickly too.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Pray, Master Barnardine, awake till you are
executed, and sleep afterwards.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Pray, Master Barnardine, awake till you are
executed, and sleep afterwards.

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text: Go in to him, and fetch him out.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
He is coming, sir, he is coming; I hear his straw
rustle.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
He is coming, sir, he is coming; I hear his straw
rustle.

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text: Is the axe upon the block, sirrah?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Very ready, sir.
Enter BARNARDINE

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text: How now, Abhorson? what's the news with you?

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text: How now, Abhorson? what's the news with you?

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text:
Truly, sir, I would desire you to clap into your
prayers; for, look you, the warrant's come.

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text:
Truly, sir, I would desire you to clap into your
prayers; for, look you, the warrant's come.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
You rogue, I have been drinking all night; I am not
fitted for 't.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
You rogue, I have been drinking all night; I am not
fitted for 't.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
O, the better, sir; for he that drinks all night,
and is hanged betimes in the morning, may sleep the
sounder all the next day.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
O, the better, sir; for he that drinks all night,
and is hanged betimes in the morning, may sleep the
sounder all the next day.
Enter DUKE VINCENTIO disguised as before

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text:
Look you, sir; here comes your ghostly father: do
we jest now, think you?

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text:
Look you, sir; here comes your ghostly father: do
we jest now, think you?

sentences.speaker: ABHORSON
sentences.text:
Look you, sir; here comes your ghostly father: do
we jest now, think you?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Sir, induced by my charity, and hearing how hastily
you are to depart, I am come to advise you, comfort
you and pray with you.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
Friar, not I. I have been drinking hard all night,
and I will have more time to prepare me, or they
shall beat out my brains with billets: I will not
consent to die this day, that's certain.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:

shall beat out my brains with billets: I will not

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
O, sir, you must: and therefore I beseech you
Look forward on the journey you shall go.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
O, sir, you must: and therefore I beseech you
Look forward on the journey you shall go.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
I swear I will not die to-day for any man's
persuasion.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: But hear you.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
Not a word: if you have any thing to say to me,
come to my ward; for thence will not I to-day.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE
sentences.text:
Not a word: if you have any thing to say to me,
come to my ward; for thence will not I to-day.

sentences.speaker: BARNARDINE

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Unfit to live or die: O gravel heart!
After him, fellows; bring him to the block.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Unfit to live or die: O gravel heart!
After him, fellows; bring him to the block.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO

Exeunt ABHORSON and POMPEY. Re-enter Provost.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: Now, sir, how do you find the prisoner?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
DUKE VINCENTIO
PROVOST
sentences.text:
Here in the prison, father,
There died this morning of a cruel fever
One Ragozine, a most notorious pirate,
A man of Claudio's years; his beard and head
Just of his colour. What if we do omit
This reprobate till he were well inclined;
And satisfy the deputy with the visage
Of Ragozine, more like to Claudio?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
O , 'tis an accident that heaven provides!
Dispatch it presently; the hour draws on
Prefix'd by Angelo: see this be done,
And sent according to command; whiles I
Persuade this rude wretch willingly to die.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:
This shall be done, good father, presently.
But Barnardine must die this afternoon:
And how shall we continue Claudio,
To save me from the danger that might come
If he were known alive?

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text:

But Barnardine must die this afternoon:

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Let this be done.
Put them in secret holds, both Barnardine and Claudio:
Ere twice the sun hath made his journal greeting
To the under generation, you shall find
Your safety manifested.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO

sentences.speaker: PROVOST
sentences.text: I am your free dependant.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Quick, dispatch, and send the head to Angelo.
Exit PROVOST

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Now will I write letters to Angelo,—
The provost, he shall bear them, whose contents
Shall witness to him I am near at home,
And that, by great injunctions, I am bound
To enter publicly: him I'll desire
To meet me at the consecrated fount
A league below the city; and from thence,
By cold gradation and well-balanced form,
We shall proceed with Angelo.

sentences.speaker: PROVOST

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
DUKE VINCENTIO
PROVOST
sentences.text: I'll make all speed.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: [Within] Peace, ho, be here!

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: [Within] Peace, ho, be here!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
The tongue of Isabel. She's come to know
If yet her brother's pardon be come hither:
But I will keep her ignorant of her good,
To make her heavenly comforts of despair,
When it is least expected.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: Ho, by your leave!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Good morning to you, fair and gracious daughter.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
The better, given me by so holy a man.
Hath yet the deputy sent my brother's pardon?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
The better, given me by so holy a man.
Hath yet the deputy sent my brother's pardon?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
He hath released him, Isabel, from the world:
His head is off and sent to Angelo.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
He hath released him, Isabel, from the world:
His head is off and sent to Angelo.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: Nay, but it is not so.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
It is no other: show your wisdom, daughter,
In your close patience.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
It is no other: show your wisdom, daughter,
In your close patience.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: O, I will to him and pluck out his eyes!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: You shall not be admitted to his sight.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
Unhappy Claudio! wretched Isabel!
Injurious world! most damned Angelo!

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:

Injurious world! most damned Angelo!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
This nor hurts him nor profits you a jot;
Forbear it therefore; give your cause to heaven.
Mark what I say, which you shall find
By every syllable a faithful verity:
The duke comes home to-morrow; nay, dry your eyes;
One of our convent, and his confessor,
Gives me this instance: already he hath carried
Notice to Escalus and Angelo,
Who do prepare to meet him at the gates,
There to give up their power. If you can, pace your
wisdom
In that good path that I would wish it go,
And you shall have your bosom on this wretch,
Grace of the duke, revenges to your heart,
And general honour.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: I am directed by you.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
This letter, then, to Friar Peter give;
'Tis that he sent me of the duke's return:
Say, by this token, I desire his company
At Mariana's house to-night. Her cause and yours
I'll perfect him withal, and he shall bring you
Before the duke, and to the head of Angelo
Accuse him home and home. For my poor self,
I am combined by a sacred vow
And shall be absent. Wend you with this letter:
Command these fretting waters from your eyes
With a light heart; trust not my holy order,
If I pervert your course. Who's here?

Enter LUCIO

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Good even. Friar, where's the provost?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Good even. Friar, where's the provost?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Not within, sir.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
O pretty Isabella, I am pale at mine heart to see
thine eyes so red: thou must be patient. I am fain
to dine and sup with water and bran; I dare not for
my head fill my belly; one fruitful meal would set
me to 't. But they say the duke will be here
to-morrow. By my troth, Isabel, I loved thy brother:
if the old fantastical duke of dark corners had been
at home, he had lived.

me to 't. But they say the duke will be here
to-morrow. By my troth, Isabel, I loved thy brother:
if the old fantastical duke of dark corners had been
at home, he had lived.
Exit ISABELLA

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Sir, the duke is marvellous little beholding to your
reports; but the best is, he lives not in them.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:

reports; but the best is, he lives not in them.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Friar, thou knowest not the duke so well as I do:
he's a better woodman than thou takest him for.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Friar, thou knowest not the duke so well as I do:
he's a better woodman than thou takest him for.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Well, you'll answer this one day. Fare ye well.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Well, you'll answer this one day. Fare ye well.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Nay, tarry; I'll go along with thee
I can tell thee pretty tales of the duke.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO

I can tell thee pretty tales of the duke.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
You have told me too many of him already, sir, if
they be true; if not true, none were enough.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
You have told me too many of him already, sir, if
they be true; if not true, none were enough.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: I was once before him for getting a wench with child.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Did you such a thing?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Yes, marry, did I but I was fain to forswear it;
they would else have married me to the rotten medlar.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:

they would else have married me to the rotten medlar.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Sir, your company is fairer than honest. Rest you well.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Sir, your company is fairer than honest. Rest you well.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO

By my troth, I'll go with thee to the lane's end:
if bawdy talk offend you, we'll have very little of
it. Nay, friar, I am a kind of burr; I shall stick.

Enter ANGELO and ESCALUS

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: Every letter he hath writ hath disvouched other.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
In most uneven and distracted manner. His actions
show much like to madness: pray heaven his wisdom be
not tainted! And why meet him at the gates, and
redeliver our authorities there?

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
In most uneven and distracted manner. His actions
show much like to madness: pray heaven his wisdom be
not tainted! And why meet him at the gates, and
redeliver our authorities there?

sentences.text:
In most uneven and distracted manner. His actions
show much like to madness: pray heaven his wisdom be

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
In most uneven and distracted manner. His actions
show much like to madness: pray heaven his wisdom be
not tainted! And why meet him at the gates, and
redeliver our authorities there?

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: I guess not.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
And why should we proclaim it in an hour before his
entering, that if any crave redress of injustice,
they should exhibit their petitions in the street?

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
He shows his reason for that: to have a dispatch of
complaints, and to deliver us from devices
hereafter, which shall then have no power to stand
against us.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
He shows his reason for that: to have a dispatch of
complaints, and to deliver us from devices
hereafter, which shall then have no power to stand
against us.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
He shows his reason for that: to have a dispatch of
complaints, and to deliver us from devices
hereafter, which shall then have no power to stand
against us.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Well, I beseech you, let it be proclaimed betimes
i' the morn; I 'll call you at your house: give
notice to such men of sort and suit as are to meet
him.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
Well, I beseech you, let it be proclaimed betimes
i' the morn; I 'll call you at your house: give

him.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: I shall, sir. Fare you well.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: I shall, sir. Fare you well.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: Good night.
Exit ESCALUS

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
This deed unshapes me quite, makes me unpregnant
And dull to all proceedings. A deflower'd maid!
And by an eminent body that enforced
The law against it! But that her tender shame
Will not proclaim against her maiden loss,
How might she tongue me! Yet reason dares her no;
For my authority bears of a credent bulk,
That no particular scandal once can touch
But it confounds the breather. He should have lived,
Save that riotous youth, with dangerous sense,
Might in the times to come have ta'en revenge,
By so receiving a dishonour'd life
With ransom of such shame. Would yet he had lived!
A lack, when once our grace we have forgot,
Nothing goes right: we would, and we would not.

Enter DUKE VINCENTIO in his own habit, and FRIAR PETER

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
These letters at fit time deliver me.
The provost knows our purpose and our plot.
The matter being afoot, keep your instruction,
And hold you ever to our special drift;
Though sometimes you do blench from this to that,
As cause doth minister. Go call at Flavius' house,
And tell him where I stay: give the like notice
To Valentinus, Rowland, and to Crassus,
And bid them bring the trumpets to the gate;
But send me Flavius first.

I thank thee, Varrius; thou hast made good haste:
Come, we will walk. There's other of our friends
Will greet us here anon, my gentle Varrius.

Enter ISABELLA and MARIANA

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
To speak so indirectly I am loath:
I would say the truth; but to accuse him so,
That is your part: yet I am advised to do it;
He says, to veil full purpose.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text: Be ruled by him.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
Besides, he tells me that, if peradventure
He speak against me on the adverse side,
I should not think it strange; for 'tis a physic
That's bitter to sweet end.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: O, peace! the friar is come.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: O, peace! the friar is come.
Enter FRIAR PETER

sentences.speaker: FRIAR PETER
sentences.text:
Come, I have found you out a stand most fit,
Where you may have such vantage on the duke,
He shall not pass you. Twice have the trumpets sounded;
The generous and gravest citizens
Have hent the gates, and very near upon
The duke is entering: therefore, hence, away!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
My very worthy cousin, fairly met!
Our old and faithful friend, we are glad to see you.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
My very worthy cousin, fairly met!
Our old and faithful friend, we are glad to see you.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: Happy return be to your royal grace!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Many and hearty thankings to you both.
We have made inquiry of you; and we hear
Such goodness of your justice, that our soul
Cannot but yield you forth to public thanks,
Forerunning more requital.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: You make my bonds still greater.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
O, your desert speaks loud; and I should wrong it,
To lock it in the wards of covert bosom,
When it deserves, with characters of brass,
A forted residence 'gainst the tooth of time
And razure of oblivion. Give me your hand,
And let the subject see, to make them know
That outward courtesies would fain proclaim
Favours that keep within. Come, Escalus,
You must walk by us on our other hand;
And good supporters are you.

FRIAR PETER and ISABELLA come forward

sentences.speaker: FRIAR PETER
sentences.text: Now is your time: speak loud and kneel before him.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
Justice, O royal duke! Vail your regard
Upon a wrong'd, I would fain have said, a maid!
O worthy prince, dishonour not your eye
By throwing it on any other object
Till you have heard me in my true complaint
And given me justice, justice, justice, justice!

Reveal yourself to him.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
O worthy duke,
You bid me seek redemption of the devil:
Hear me yourself; for that which I must speak
Must either punish me, not being believed,
Or wring redress from you. Hear me, O hear me, here!

sentences.text:
My lord, her wits, I fear me, are not firm:
She hath been a suitor to me for her brother
Cut off by course of justice,—

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
My lord, her wits, I fear me, are not firm:
She hath been a suitor to me for her brother
Cut off by course of justice,—

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: By course of justice!

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: And she will speak most bitterly and strange.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
Most strange, but yet most truly, will I speak:
That Angelo's forsworn; is it not strange?
That Angelo's a murderer; is 't not strange?
That Angelo is an adulterous thief,
An hypocrite, a virgin-violator;
Is it not strange and strange?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Nay, it is ten times strange.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
It is not truer he is Angelo
Than this is all as true as it is strange:
Nay, it is ten times true; for truth is truth
To the end of reckoning.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
It is not truer he is Angelo

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Away with her! Poor soul,
She speaks this in the infirmity of sense.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Away with her! Poor soul,
She speaks this in the infirmity of sense.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
O prince, I conjure thee, as thou believest
There is another comfort than this world,
That thou neglect me not, with that opinion
That I am touch'd with madness! Make not impossible
That which but seems unlike: 'tis not impossible
But one, the wicked'st caitiff on the ground,
May seem as shy, as grave, as just, as absolute
As Angelo; even so may Angelo,
In all his dressings, caracts, titles, forms,
Be an arch-villain; believe it, royal prince:
If he be less, he's nothing; but he's more,
Had I more name for badness.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
By mine honesty,
If she be mad,—as I believe no other,—
Her madness hath the oddest frame of sense,
Such a dependency of thing on thing,
As e'er I heard in madness.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
By mine honesty,

Her madness hath the oddest frame of sense,

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
O gracious duke,
Harp not on that, nor do not banish reason
For inequality; but let your reason serve
To make the truth appear where it seems hid,
And hide the false seems true.

sentences.text:
Many that are not mad
Have, sure, more lack of reason. What would you say?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
I am the sister of one Claudio,
Condemn'd upon the act of fornication
To lose his head; condemn'd by Angelo:
I, in probation of a sisterhood,
Was sent to by my brother; one Lucio
As then the messenger,—

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
I am the sister of one Claudio,
Condemn'd upon the act of fornication
To lose his head; condemn'd by Angelo:
I, in probation of a sisterhood,
Was sent to by my brother; one Lucio
As then the messenger,—

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
That's I, an't like your grace:
I came to her from Claudio, and desired her
To try her gracious fortune with Lord Angelo
For her poor brother's pardon.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
That's I, an't like your grace:
I came to her from Claudio, and desired her
To try her gracious fortune with Lord Angelo
For her poor brother's pardon.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: That's he indeed.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: You were not bid to speak.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
No, my good lord;
Nor wish'd to hold my peace.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
I wish you now, then;
Pray you, take note of it: and when you have
A business for yourself, pray heaven you then
Be perfect.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
I wish you now, then;
Pray you, take note of it: and when you have
A business for yourself, pray heaven you then
Be perfect.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: I warrant your honour.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: The warrants for yourself; take heed to't.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: This gentleman told somewhat of my tale,—

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Right.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
It may be right; but you are i' the wrong
To speak before your time. Proceed.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
I went
To this pernicious caitiff deputy,—

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: That's somewhat madly spoken.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
Pardon it;
The phrase is to the matter.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
Pardon it;
The phrase is to the matter.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Mended again. The matter; proceed.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Mended again. The matter; proceed.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
In brief, to set the needless process by,
How I persuaded, how I pray'd, and kneel'd,
How he refell'd me, and how I replied,—
For this was of much length, —the vile conclusion
I now begin with grief and shame to utter:
He would not, but by gift of my chaste body
To his concupiscible intemperate lust,
Release my brother; and, after much debatement,
My sisterly remorse confutes mine honour,
And I did yield to him: but the next morn betimes,
His purpose surfeiting, he sends a warrant
For my poor brother's head.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: This is most likely!

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: O, that it were as like as it is true!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
By heaven, fond wretch, thou knowist not what thou
speak'st,
Or else thou art suborn'd against his honour
In hateful practise. First, his integrity
Stands without blemish. Next, it imports no reason
That with such vehemency he should pursue
Faults proper to himself: if he had so offended,
He would have weigh'd thy brother by himself
And not have cut him off. Some one hath set you on:
Confess the truth, and say by whose advice
Thou camest here to complain.

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text:
And is this all?
Then, O you blessed ministers above,
Keep me in patience, and with ripen'd time
Unfold the evil which is here wrapt up
In countenance! Heaven shield your grace from woe,
As I, thus wrong'd, hence unbelieved go!

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
I know you'ld fain be gone. An officer!
To prison with her! Shall we thus permit
A blasting and a scandalous breath to fall
On him so near us? This needs must be a practise.
Who knew of Your intent and coming hither?

sentences.speaker: ISABELLA
sentences.text: One that I would were here, Friar Lodowick.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: A ghostly father, belike. Who knows that Lodowick?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: A ghostly father, belike. Who knows that Lodowick?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
My lord, I know him; 'tis a meddling friar;
I do not like the man: had he been lay, my lord
For certain words he spake against your grace
In your retirement, I had swinged him soundly.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Words against me? this is a good friar, belike!
And to set on this wretched woman here
Against our substitute! Let this friar be found.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
But yesternight, my lord, she and that friar,
I saw them at the prison: a saucy friar,
A very scurvy fellow.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR PETER
sentences.text:
Blessed be your royal grace!
I have stood by, my lord, and I have heard
Your royal ear abused. First, hath this woman
Most wrongfully accused your substitute,
Who is as free from touch or soil with her
As she from one ungot.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
We did believe no less.
Know you that Friar Lodowick that she speaks of?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
We did believe no less.
Know you that Friar Lodowick that she speaks of?

sentences.speaker: FRIAR PETER
FRIAR PETER
LUCIO
sentences.text: My lord, most villanously; believe it.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: My lord, most villanously; believe it.

sentences.speaker: FRIAR PETER
sentences.text:
Well, he in time may come to clear himself;
But at this instant he is sick my lord,
Of a strange fever. Upon his mere request,
Being come to knowledge that there was complaint
Intended 'gainst Lord Angelo, came I hither,
To speak, as from his mouth, what he doth know
Is true and false; and what he with his oath
And all probation will make up full clear,
Whensoever he's convented. First, for this woman.
To justify this worthy nobleman,
So vulgarly and personally accused,
Her shall you hear disproved to her eyes,
Till she herself confess it.

sentences.text:
Well, he in time may come to clear himself;
But at this instant he is sick my lord,
Of a strange fever. Upon his mere request,
Being come to knowledge that there was complaint
Intended 'gainst Lord Angelo, came I hither,
To speak, as from his mouth, what he doth know
Is true and false; and what he with his oath
And all probation will make up full clear,
Whensoever he's convented. First, for this woman.
To justify this worthy nobleman,
So vulgarly and personally accused,
Her shall you hear disproved to her eyes,
Till she herself confess it.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Good friar, let's hear it.
ISABELLA is carried off guarded and MARIANA comes forward

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Do you not smile at this, Lord Angelo?
O heaven, the vanity of wretched fools!
Give us some seats. Come, cousin Angelo;
In this I'll be impartial; be you judge
Of your own cause. Is this the witness, friar?
First, let her show her face, and after speak.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
Pardon, my lord; I will not show my face
Until my husband bid me.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: What, are you married?

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text: No, my lord.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Are you a maid?

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text: No, my lord.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: A widow, then?

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text: Neither, my lord.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Why, you are nothing then: neither maid, widow, nor
wife?

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Why, you are nothing then: neither maid, widow, nor
wife?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
My lord, she may be a punk; for many of them are
neither maid, widow, nor wife.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Silence that fellow: I would he had some cause
To prattle for himself.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Silence that fellow: I would he had some cause

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Well, my lord.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
My lord; I do confess I ne'er was married;
And I confess besides I am no maid:
I have known my husband; yet my husband
Knows not that ever he knew me.

sentences.text: He was drunk then, my lord: it can be no better.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: For the benefit of silence, would thou wert so too!

sentences.text: Well, my lord.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: This is no witness for Lord Angelo.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
Now I come to't my lord
She that accuses him of fornication,
In self-same manner doth accuse my husband,
And charges him my lord, with such a time
When I'll depose I had him in mine arms
With all the effect of love.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text: Not that I know.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: No? you say your husband.

sentences.text: No? you say your husband.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
Why, just, my lord, and that is Angelo,
Who thinks he knows that he ne'er knew my body,
But knows he thinks that he knows Isabel's.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: This is a strange abuse. Let's see thy face.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text: This is a strange abuse. Let's see thy face.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
My husband bids me; now I will unmask.
[Unveiling]
This is that face, thou cruel Angelo,
Which once thou sworest was worth the looking on;
This is the hand which, with a vow'd contract,
Was fast belock'd in thine; this is the body
That took away the match from Isabel,
And did supply thee at thy garden-house
In her imagined person.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Know you this woman?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Carnally, she says.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Sirrah, no more!

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Enough, my lord.

sentences.speaker: ANGELO
sentences.text:
My lord, I must confess I know this woman:
And five years since there was some speech of marriage
Betwixt myself and her; which was broke off,
Partly for that her promised proportions
Came short of composition, but in chief
For that her reputation was disvalued
In levity: since which time of five years
I never spake with her, saw her, nor heard from her,
Upon my faith and honour.

sentences.speaker: MARIANA
sentences.text:
Noble prince,
As there comes light from heaven and words from breath,
As there is sense in truth and truth in virtue,
I am affianced this man's wife as strongly
As words could make up vows: and, my good lord,
But Tuesday night last gone in's garden-house
He knew me as a wife. As this is true,
Let me in safety raise me from my knees
Or else for ever be confixed here,
A marble monument!

I did but smile till now:
Now, good my lord, give me the scope of justice
My patience here is touch'd. I do perceive
These poor informal women are no more
But instruments of some more mightier member
That sets them on: let me have way, my lord,
To find this practise out.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Ay, with my heart
And punish them to your height of pleasure.
Thou foolish friar, and thou pernicious woman,
Compact with her that's gone, think'st thou thy oaths,
Though they would swear down each particular saint,
Were testimonies against his worth and credit
That's seal'd in approbation? You, Lord Escalus,
Sit with my cousin; lend him your kind pains
To find out this abuse, whence 'tis derived.
There is another friar that set them on;
Let him be sent for.

Would he were here, my lord! for he indeed
Hath set the women on to this complaint:
Your provost knows the place where he abides
And he may fetch him.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text: Go do it instantly.
Exit Provost

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
And you, my noble and well-warranted cousin,
Whom it concerns to hear this matter forth,
Do with your injuries as seems you best,
In any chastisement: I for a while will leave you;
But stir not you till you have well determined
Upon these slanderers.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: My lord, we'll do it throughly.
Exit DUKE

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Signior Lucio, did not you say you knew that
Friar Lodowick to be a dishonest person?

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Signior Lucio, did not you say you knew that
Friar Lodowick to be a dishonest person?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
"Cucullus non facit monachum:" honest in nothing
but in his clothes; and one that hath spoke most
villanous speeches of the duke.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
"Cucullus non facit monachum:" honest in nothing
but in his clothes; and one that hath spoke most
villanous speeches of the duke.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
We shall entreat you to abide here till he come and
enforce them against him: we shall find this friar a
notable fellow.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
We shall entreat you to abide here till he come and

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: As any in Vienna, on my word.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Call that same Isabel here once again; I would speak
with her.
Exit an Attendant

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Pray you, my lord, give me leave to question; you
shall see how I'll handle her.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Pray you, my lord, give me leave to question; you
shall see how I'll handle her.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text: Not better than he, by her own report.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: Say you?

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
sentences.text:
Marry, sir, I think, if you handled her privately,
she would sooner confess: perchance, publicly,
she'll be ashamed.

Come on, mistress: here's a gentlewoman denies all
that you have said.

My lord, here comes the rascal I spoke of; here with

In very good time: speak not you to him till we

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
Come, sir: did you set these women on to slander
Lord Angelo? they have confessed you did.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text: How! know you where you are?

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Respect to your great place! and let the devil
Be sometime honour'd for his burning throne!
Where is the duke? 'tis he should hear me speak.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
The duke's in us; and we will hear you speak:
Look you speak justly.

sentences.speaker: ESCALUS
sentences.text:
The duke's in us; and we will hear you speak:
Look you speak justly.

sentences.speaker: DUKE VINCENTIO
sentences.text:
Boldly, at least. But, O, poor souls,
Come you to seek the lamb here of the fox?
Good night to your redress! Is the duke gone?
Then is your cause gone too. The duke's unjust,
Thus to retort your manifest appeal,
And put your trial in the villain's mouth
Which here you come to accuse.

sentences.speaker: LUCIO
